library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(janitor)
library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(haven)
library(ranger)
library(glmnet)
library(xgboost)
library(Metrics)
library(knitr)

Part 1: Data Cleaning

1.1 Target Variables: Annual change in log carbon intensity

### Calculating Scope 1 Carbon Emissions ###

dir_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/target_variable"

# List all Emission Inventories Excel files in the folder
files <- list.files(
  dir_path,
  pattern = "Emission_Inventories.*\\.xlsx$",
  full.names = TRUE,
  ignore.case = TRUE
)
cat("Files found:", length(files), "\n")
Files found: 15 
# Extract province name from sheet name by removing trailing 4-digit year
prov_from_sheet <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("\\s+", "", x)
  sub("\\d{4}$", "", x)
}

# For each file, iterate over province sheets and extract Scope 1 Total Emissions
extract_scope1 <- function(file) {
  year <- as.integer(str_extract(basename(file), "\\d{4}"))
  sheets <- excel_sheets(file)
  
  # Skip the NOTE sheet (metadata only)
  sheets <- sheets[sheets != "NOTE"]
  
  map_dfr(sheets, function(sh) {
    tryCatch({
      df <- read_excel(file, sheet = sh, col_names = TRUE)
      df <- clean_names(df)
      
      # Ensure first column is named emission_inventory
      if (!"emission_inventory" %in% names(df)) names(df)[1] <- "emission_inventory"
      
      # Find the TotalEmissions row (strip spaces and lowercase for robust matching)
      row_idx <- which(
        tolower(gsub("\\s+", "", as.character(df$emission_inventory))) == "totalemissions"
      )
      
      # Find the Scope_1_Total column; fall back to regex if exact match fails
      cand <- which(tolower(names(df)) == "scope_1_total")
      if (length(cand) == 0)
        cand <- grep("scope.*1.*total", tolower(names(df)), perl = TRUE)
      
      # Skip sheet if either the row or column cannot be located
      if (length(cand) == 0 || length(row_idx) == 0) return(NULL)
      
      tibble(
        province     = prov_from_sheet(sh),
        year         = year,
        scope1_total = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[cand[1]]][row_idx[1]]))
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      message("Error in sheet ", sh, ": ", e$message)
      NULL
    })
  })
}

# Apply extraction across all files and combine into one data frame
final_scope1 <- map_dfr(files, extract_scope1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(scope1_total)) %>%
  arrange(province, year)

# Save output to CSV
out_file <- file.path(dir_path, "Scope1_Total_Emissions_2008_2022.csv")
write.csv(final_scope1, out_file, row.names = FALSE)

# Preview results
print(head(final_scope1, 10))

 8 Anhui     2015         364.
 9 Anhui     2016         376.
10 Anhui     2017         382.
# Check number of provinces per year (should be ~30 each year)
final_scope1 %>%
  count(year) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  print(n = 50)

### Carbon Intensity Calculation ###
emissions <- final_scope1 

# Load Provincial GDP
gdp_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/economic_structure/provincial_gdp_2008_2021.xlsx"

gdp_wide <- read_excel(gdp_path, skip = 2) 

# Reshape from wide to long (province, year, gdp)
gdp_long <- gdp_wide %>%
  rename(province = 1) %>%                   
  filter(!is.na(province)) %>%               
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -province,
    names_to  = "year",
    values_to = "gdp_100m_yuan"              # Unit: 100 million yuan
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Harmonize province names between the two datasets
# Check for mismatches before joining
emissions_provs <- sort(unique(emissions$province))
gdp_provs       <- sort(unique(gdp_long$province))

cat("Provinces only in emissions:\n")
Provinces only in emissions:
print(setdiff(emissions_provs, gdp_provs))
[1] "InnerMongolia"
cat("Provinces only in GDP:\n")
Provinces only in GDP:
print(setdiff(gdp_provs, emissions_provs))
[1] "Inner Mongolia" "Tibet"         
# Fix the known mismatch
emissions <- emissions %>%
  mutate(province = recode(province, "InnerMongolia" = "Inner Mongolia"))

# Merge and calculate carbon intensity
carbon_intensity <- emissions %>%
  left_join(gdp_long, by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(gdp_100m_yuan)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Carbon intensity: Mt CO2 per 100 million yuan GDP
    ci       = scope1_total / gdp_100m_yuan,
    
    # Log carbon intensity
    ln_ci    = log(ci),
    
    # Annual change in log carbon intensity (target variable)
    d_ln_ci  = ln_ci - lag(ln_ci, 1)
  ) %>%
  
  # lag() must be applied within each province
  group_by(province) %>%
  arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(d_ln_ci = ln_ci - lag(ln_ci, 1)) %>%
  ungroup()

print(head(carbon_intensity, 10))
# A tibble: 10 × 7

10 Anhui     2017         382.        30142. 0.0127 -4.37 -0.101

out_ci <- file.path(econ_path, "carbon_intensity_2008_2021.csv")

1.2 Control Variables

### GDP per Capita Calculation ###
econ_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/economic_structure"

# Load GDP data
gdp_wide <- read_excel(
  file.path(econ_path, "provincial_gdp_2008_2021.xlsx"),
  skip = 2  
)

gdp_long <- gdp_wide %>%
  rename(province = 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(province), province != "Province") %>%  
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -province,
    names_to  = "year",
    values_to = "gdp_100m_yuan"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Load Population data (unit: 10,000 persons) 
province_order <- c(
  "Beijing", "Tianjin", "Hebei", "Shanxi", "Inner Mongolia",
  "Liaoning", "Jilin", "Heilongjiang",
  "Shanghai", "Jiangsu", "Zhejiang", "Anhui", "Fujian", "Jiangxi", "Shandong",
  "Henan", "Hubei", "Hunan", "Guangdong", "Guangxi", "Hainan",
  "Chongqing", "Sichuan", "Guizhou", "Yunnan", "Tibet",
  "Shaanxi", "Gansu", "Qinghai", "Ningxia", "Xinjiang"
)

pop_wide <- read_xls(
  file.path(econ_path, "provincial_population_2008_2021.xls"),
  skip = 0   # Keep row 0 as header (Region, 2021, 2020, ..., 2008)
)

pop_long <- pop_wide %>%
  select(-1) %>%                            # Drop the "Region" placeholder column
  mutate(province = province_order) %>%     # Assign province names by row position
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -province,
    names_to  = "year",
    values_to = "population_10k"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Calculate GDP per capita and log GDP per capita
gdp_percap <- gdp_long %>%
  left_join(pop_long, by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(population_10k), province != "Tibet") %>% 
  mutate(
    # GDP per capita in yuan: (100m yuan × 1e8) / (10k persons × 1e4)
    gdp_percap    = gdp_100m_yuan * 10000 / population_10k,
    ln_gdp_percap = log(gdp_percap)
  ) %>%
  arrange(province, year)

print(head(gdp_percap, 10))
# A tibble: 10 × 6
   province  year gdp_100m_yuan population_10k gdp_percap ln_gdp_percap
   <chr>    <int>         <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>

 2 Anhui     2009        11063.           6131     18045.          9.80
 3 Anhui     2010        13475.           5957     22620.         10.0 
 4 Anhui     2011        16533.           5972     27684.         10.2 
 5 Anhui     2012        18614.           5978     31137.         10.3 
 6 Anhui     2013        20888.           5988     34883.         10.5 
 7 Anhui     2014        22881.           5997     38154.         10.5 
 8 Anhui     2015        24143.           6011     40164.         10.6 
 9 Anhui     2016        26801.           6033     44424.         10.7 
10 Anhui     2017        30142.           6057     49764.         10.8 
cat("\nRows:", nrow(gdp_percap), "\n")  

Rows: 420 
out_gdp <- file.path(econ_path, "gdp_percap_2008_2021.csv")
write.csv(gdp_percap, out_gdp, row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved to:", out_gdp, "\n")
Saved to: D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/economic_structure/gdp_percap_2008_2021.csv

Part 2: Merge All Data and Run Models

2.1 Data Merging

# Define paths
econ_path   <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/economic_structure"
energy_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/energy_structure"
policy_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/policy"
target_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/target_variable"

# Load each dataset
# Target variable
ci <- read.csv(file.path(target_path, "carbon_intensity_2008_2021.csv")) %>%
  select(province, year, ln_ci, d_ln_ci)

# GDP per capita
gdp_pc <- read.csv(file.path(econ_path, "gdp_percap_2008_2021.csv")) %>%
  select(province, year, ln_gdp_percap)

# Urban population rate
urban <- read_excel(file.path(econ_path, "urban_population_rate.xlsx"), skip = 2) %>%
  rename(province = 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(province)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "year", values_to = "urban_rate") %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Secondary industry share
sh_sec <- read_excel(
  file.path(econ_path, "industry_shares_2008_2021.xlsx"),
  sheet = "Secondary Industry Share", skip = 2
) %>%
  rename(province = 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(province)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "year", values_to = "sh_secondary") %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Tertiary industry share
sh_ter <- read_excel(
  file.path(econ_path, "industry_shares_2008_2021.xlsx"),
  sheet = "Tertiary Industry Share", skip = 2
) %>%
  rename(province = 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(province)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-province, names_to = "year", values_to = "sh_tertiary") %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Coal share
coal <- read.csv(file.path(energy_path, "coal_share.csv")) %>%
  select(province, year, coal_share) %>%
  mutate(province = recode(province, "Inner mongolia" = "Inner Mongolia"))

# Policy intensity — filter to 2008-2021, drop Xizang
policy <- read_dta(file.path(policy_path, "Provincial_Policy_Intensity.dta")) %>%
  filter(year >= 2008, year <= 2021, province != "Xizang") %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# Merge all into one panel
panel <- ci %>%
  left_join(gdp_pc,  by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  left_join(urban,   by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  left_join(sh_sec,  by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  left_join(sh_ter,  by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  left_join(coal,    by = c("province", "year")) %>%
  left_join(policy,  by = c("province", "year"))

cat("Panel rows after merge:", nrow(panel), "\n")
Panel rows after merge: 420 
print(head(panel, 5))
  province year     ln_ci     d_ln_ci ln_gdp_percap urban_rate sh_secondary
1    Anhui 2008 -3.762797          NA      9.667549      40.50         46.6
2    Anhui 2009 -3.783889 -0.02109250      9.800616      42.10         48.7
3    Anhui 2010 -3.904817 -0.12092761     10.026580      43.01         52.1
4    Anhui 2011 -4.029223 -0.12440597     10.228599      44.80         54.3
5    Anhui 2012 -4.050921 -0.02169835     10.346152      46.50         54.6
  sh_tertiary coal_share PI_provincial
1        37.4  0.9350248          13.5
2        36.4  0.9370993          30.5
3        33.9  0.9337997          37.5
4        32.5  0.9332939          12.5
5        32.7  0.9434895          14.0

2.2 Feature Engineering and Train/Test Split

# Construct lagged features (t-1 predicts t)
features <- c("ln_gdp_percap", "urban_rate", "sh_secondary",
              "sh_tertiary", "coal_share", "PI_provincial", "ln_ci")

panel_lagged <- panel %>%
  group_by(province) %>%
  arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(features), ~ lag(., 1), .names = "{.col}_lag")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(d_ln_ci), !is.na(ln_gdp_percap_lag))

cat("Rows after lagging (model-ready):", nrow(panel_lagged), "\n")
Rows after lagging (model-ready): 390 
lag_features <- paste0(features, "_lag")

# Train (2009-2017) / Test (2018-2021) split
train <- panel_lagged %>% filter(year <= 2017)
test  <- panel_lagged %>% filter(year >= 2018)

cat("Train rows:", nrow(train), " | Test rows:", nrow(test), "\n")
Train rows: 270  | Test rows: 120 
X_train <- as.matrix(train[, lag_features])
y_train <- train$d_ln_ci
X_test  <- as.matrix(test[, lag_features])
y_test  <- test$d_ln_ci

2.3 Model 0: Naïve Persistence Baseline

# Predict next year's change using last year's log carbon intensity change
# i.e., assume d_ln_ci(t) ≈ d_ln_ci(t-1)
t_naive <- system.time({
  naive_pred <- panel_lagged %>%
    group_by(province) %>%
    arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(naive_pred = lag(d_ln_ci, 1)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(year >= 2018, !is.na(naive_pred))
})["elapsed"]

# Align y_test to matched rows (some rows may drop due to missing lag)
naive_actual <- naive_pred$d_ln_ci
naive_hat    <- naive_pred$naive_pred

cat("\n--- Naïve Persistence Baseline ---\n")

--- Naïve Persistence Baseline ---
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(naive_actual,  naive_hat), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.0566 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(naive_actual, naive_hat), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0745 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(naive_actual,  naive_hat)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.0046

2.4 Model 1: OLS

train_df <- data.frame(d_ln_ci = y_train, X_train)
ols_formula <- as.formula(paste("d_ln_ci ~", paste(lag_features, collapse = " + ")))
ols_model <- lm(ols_formula, data = train_df)

t_ols <- system.time({
  ols_pred <- predict(ols_model, newdata = as.data.frame(X_test))
})["elapsed"]

cat("\n--- OLS Results ---\n")

--- OLS Results ---
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(y_test, ols_pred), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.05 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(y_test, ols_pred), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0612 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(y_test, ols_pred)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.0661

2.5 Model 2: Ridge Regression

# Use cross-validation to select optimal lambda
t_ridge <- system.time({
  set.seed(42)
  ridge_cv     <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
  ridge_lambda <- ridge_cv$lambda.min
  ridge_model  <- glmnet(X_train, y_train, alpha = 0, lambda = ridge_lambda)
})["elapsed"]

ridge_pred <- as.vector(predict(ridge_model, newx = X_test, s = ridge_lambda))

cat("\n--- Ridge Regression ---\n")

--- Ridge Regression ---
cat("Optimal lambda:", round(ridge_lambda, 5), "\n")
Optimal lambda: 0.01009 
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(y_test, ridge_pred), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.0487 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(y_test, ridge_pred), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0599 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(y_test, ridge_pred)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.0686

2.6 Model 3: Lasso Regression

set.seed(42)
t_lasso <- system.time({
  set.seed(42)
  lasso_cv     <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
  lasso_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min
  lasso_model  <- glmnet(X_train, y_train, alpha = 1, lambda = lasso_lambda)
})["elapsed"]

lasso_pred <- as.vector(predict(lasso_model, newx = X_test, s = lasso_lambda))

# Show which features Lasso retained
lasso_coef <- coef(lasso_model, s = lasso_lambda)
cat("\n--- Lasso Regression ---\n")

--- Lasso Regression ---
cat("Optimal lambda:", round(lasso_lambda, 5), "\n")
Optimal lambda: 0.0014 
cat("Non-zero coefficients:\n")
Non-zero coefficients:
print(lasso_coef[lasso_coef[, 1] != 0, , drop = FALSE])
6 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
                  s=0.001397589
(Intercept)        0.1197342394
sh_secondary_lag   0.0002427191
sh_tertiary_lag   -0.0006803796
coal_share_lag    -0.0639507313
PI_provincial_lag -0.0004425271
ln_ci_lag          0.0273188923
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(y_test, lasso_pred), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.0484 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(y_test, lasso_pred), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0596 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(y_test, lasso_pred)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.0641

2.7 Model 4: Random Forest

train_rf <- data.frame(d_ln_ci = y_train, X_train)

t_rf <- system.time({
  train_rf <- data.frame(d_ln_ci = y_train, X_train)
  rf_model <- ranger(
    formula    = d_ln_ci ~ .,
    data       = train_rf,
    num.trees  = 500,
    importance = "permutation",
    seed       = 42
  )
})["elapsed"]

rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, data = as.data.frame(X_test))$predictions

cat("\n--- Random Forest ---\n")

--- Random Forest ---
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(y_test, rf_pred), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.0453 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(y_test, rf_pred), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0579 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(y_test, rf_pred)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.0496

2.8 Model 5: XGBoost

# Cross-validation to find optimal number of rounds
t_xgb <- system.time({
  set.seed(42)
  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_train, label = y_train)
  xgb_params <- list(
    objective        = "reg:squarederror",
    eta              = 0.05,
    max_depth        = 4,
    subsample        = 0.8,
    colsample_bytree = 0.8
  )
  xgb_model <- xgb.train(
    params  = xgb_params,
    data    = dtrain,
    nrounds = 100,
    verbose = 0
  )
})["elapsed"]

dtest    <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_test, label = y_test)
xgb_pred <- predict(xgb_model, dtest)

cat("\n--- XGBoost ---\n")

--- XGBoost ---
cat("MAE: ",  round(mae(y_test, xgb_pred), 4), "\n")
MAE:  0.0451 
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse(y_test, xgb_pred), 4), "\n")
RMSE: 0.0577 
cat("R²:  ",  round(cor(y_test, xgb_pred)^2, 4), "\n")
R²:   0.042

2.9 Model Comparison Summary

# Align naïve baseline to test set province-years for consistent comparison
# (naïve may lose one row per province due to double-lag)
naive_test_rows <- naive_pred %>%
  select(province, year, d_ln_ci, naive_pred)

# Collect all predictions on the common test set
results_all <- test %>%
  select(province, year, d_ln_ci) %>%
  mutate(
    ols_pred   = ols_pred,
    ridge_pred = ridge_pred,
    lasso_pred = lasso_pred,
    rf_pred    = rf_pred,
    xgb_pred   = xgb_pred
  )

# Build comparison table
model_names <- c("Naïve Persistence", "OLS", "Ridge", "Lasso",
                 "Random Forest", "XGBoost")

calc_metrics <- function(actual, pred) {
  c(
    MAE  = round(mae(actual, pred), 4),
    RMSE = round(rmse(actual, pred), 4),
    R2   = round(cor(actual, pred)^2, 4)
  )
}

metrics_table <- rbind(
  calc_metrics(naive_actual,      naive_hat),
  calc_metrics(y_test,            ols_pred),
  calc_metrics(y_test,            ridge_pred),
  calc_metrics(y_test,            lasso_pred),
  calc_metrics(y_test,            rf_pred),
  calc_metrics(y_test,            xgb_pred)
) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(Model = model_names) %>%
  select(Model, MAE, RMSE, R2)

kable(metrics_table,
      caption = "Out-of-sample Performance Comparison (Test Set: 2018–2021)",
      align   = c("l", "r", "r", "r"))
Out-of-sample Performance Comparison (Test Set: 2018–2021)
Model MAE RMSE R2
Naïve Persistence 0.0566 0.0745 0.0046
OLS 0.0500 0.0612 0.0661
Ridge 0.0487 0.0599 0.0686
Lasso 0.0484 0.0596 0.0641
Random Forest 0.0453 0.0579 0.0496
XGBoost 0.0451 0.0577 0.0420 
# Save all predictions for use in Part 3
saveRDS(list(
  results_all  = results_all,
  rf_model     = rf_model,
  xgb_model    = xgb_model,
  lasso_model  = lasso_model,
  ridge_model  = ridge_model,
  ols_model    = ols_model,
  X_train      = X_train,
  X_test       = X_test,
  y_test       = y_test,
  lag_features = lag_features,
  metrics_table = metrics_table
), file = file.path(target_path, "model_objects.rds"))

cat("\nModel objects saved to model_objects.rds for use in Part 3.\n")

Model objects saved to model_objects.rds for use in Part 3.

Part 3: Model Interpretation and Comparative Analysis

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(ranger)
library(xgboost)
library(glmnet)
library(shapviz)
library(patchwork)

# Load model objects saved at end of Part 2
target_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/target_variable"
obj <- readRDS(file.path(target_path, "model_objects.rds"))

results_all  <- obj$results_all
rf_model     <- obj$rf_model
xgb_model    <- obj$xgb_model
lasso_model  <- obj$lasso_model
ridge_model  <- obj$ridge_model
ols_model    <- obj$ols_model
X_train      <- obj$X_train
X_test       <- obj$X_test
y_test       <- obj$y_test
lag_features <- obj$lag_features
metrics_table <- obj$metrics_table

# Clean feature labels for plotting
feature_labels <- c(
  ln_gdp_percap_lag  = "Log GDP per Capita",
  urban_rate_lag     = "Urbanization Rate",
  sh_secondary_lag   = "Secondary Industry Share",
  sh_tertiary_lag    = "Tertiary Industry Share",
  coal_share_lag     = "Coal Share",
  PI_provincial_lag  = "Policy Intensity",
  ln_ci_lag          = "Log Carbon Intensity (lag)"
)

3.1 Prediction Performance: Actual vs. Predicted

# Reshape to long format for faceted plot
pred_long <- results_all %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = ends_with("_pred"),
    names_to  = "model",
    values_to = "predicted"
  ) %>%
  mutate(model = recode(model,
    ols_pred   = "OLS",
    ridge_pred = "Ridge",
    lasso_pred = "Lasso",
    rf_pred    = "Random Forest",
    xgb_pred   = "XGBoost"
  ))

ggplot(pred_long, aes(x = d_ln_ci, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ model, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title    = "Actual vs. Predicted Carbon Intensity Change (Test Set: 2018–2021)",
    subtitle = "Dashed red line = perfect prediction",
    x        = "Actual Δln(CI)",
    y        = "Predicted Δln(CI)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

3.2 Prediction Error Distribution

# Residuals for each model
residuals_long <- results_all %>%
  mutate(
    OLS           = d_ln_ci - ols_pred,
    Ridge         = d_ln_ci - ridge_pred,
    Lasso         = d_ln_ci - lasso_pred,
    `Random Forest` = d_ln_ci - rf_pred,
    XGBoost       = d_ln_ci - xgb_pred
  ) %>%
  select(province, year, OLS, Ridge, Lasso, `Random Forest`, XGBoost) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(province, year), names_to = "model", values_to = "residual") %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model,
    levels = c("OLS", "Ridge", "Lasso", "Random Forest", "XGBoost")))

ggplot(residuals_long, aes(x = model, y = residual, fill = model)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Prediction Errors by Model",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Residual (Actual − Predicted)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

3.3 OLS and Lasso Coefficients

# OLS coefficients with confidence intervals
ols_summary <- summary(ols_model)$coefficients %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  filter(feature != "(Intercept)") %>%
  rename(estimate = Estimate, se = `Std. Error`, p_value = `Pr(>|t|)`) %>%
  mutate(
    lower    = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    upper    = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    feature  = recode(feature, !!!feature_labels),
    sig      = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "p < 0.05", "p ≥ 0.05")
  )

p_ols <- ggplot(ols_summary, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(feature, estimate), color = sig)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.3) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("p < 0.05" = "#d73027", "p ≥ 0.05" = "#91bfdb")) +
  labs(title = "OLS Coefficients (95% CI)", x = "Coefficient", y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Lasso coefficients
lasso_coef_df <- coef(lasso_model) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  rename(estimate = s0) %>%
  filter(feature != "(Intercept)", estimate != 0) %>%
  mutate(feature = recode(feature, !!!feature_labels))

p_lasso <- ggplot(lasso_coef_df, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(feature, estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_col(fill = "#4dac26", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Lasso Coefficients (Non-zero Only)", x = "Coefficient", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

p_ols + p_lasso

3.4 Feature Importance: Random Forest (Permutation)

rf_imp <- rf_model$variable.importance %>%
  enframe(name = "feature", value = "importance") %>%
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature, !!!feature_labels),
    importance_pct = importance / sum(importance) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(importance))

ggplot(rf_imp, aes(x = importance_pct, y = reorder(feature, importance_pct))) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", importance_pct)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Random Forest: Permutation Feature Importance",
    x     = "Importance (%)",
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

3.5 Feature Importance: XGBoost (Gain)

xgb_imp <- xgb.importance(
  feature_names = lag_features,
  model         = xgb_model
) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(
    Feature    = recode(Feature, !!!feature_labels),
    Gain_pct   = Gain / sum(Gain) * 100
  )

ggplot(xgb_imp, aes(x = Gain_pct, y = reorder(Feature, Gain_pct))) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Gain_pct)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "XGBoost: Feature Importance (Gain)",
    x     = "Gain (%)",
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

3.6 Feature Importance Comparison Across Models

# Normalize all importances to 0–1 scale for cross-model comparison

# OLS: use absolute t-statistic as proxy for importance
ols_imp <- summary(ols_model)$coefficients %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  filter(feature != "(Intercept)") %>%
  transmute(
    feature    = recode(feature, !!!feature_labels),
    importance = abs(`t value`)
  ) %>%
  mutate(importance = importance / sum(importance), model = "OLS (|t-stat|)")

# Lasso: absolute coefficients
lasso_imp <- lasso_coef_df %>%
  transmute(
    feature    = feature,
    importance = abs(estimate)
  ) %>%
  # Fill in zeroed-out features
  right_join(
    tibble(feature = unname(feature_labels)),
    by = "feature"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    importance = replace_na(importance, 0),
    importance = importance / sum(importance),
    model      = "Lasso (|coef|)"
  )

# RF: permutation importance (already normalized above)
rf_imp2 <- rf_imp %>%
  transmute(feature, importance = importance / sum(importance), model = "Random Forest")

# XGBoost: gain
xgb_imp2 <- xgb_imp %>%
  transmute(feature = Feature, importance = Gain / sum(Gain), model = "XGBoost")

importance_combined <- bind_rows(ols_imp, lasso_imp, rf_imp2, xgb_imp2) %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model,
    levels = c("OLS (|t-stat|)", "Lasso (|coef|)", "Random Forest", "XGBoost")))

ggplot(importance_combined,
       aes(x = importance, y = reorder(feature, importance), fill = model)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "Feature Importance Comparison Across Models",
    x     = "Normalized Importance",
    y     = NULL,
    fill  = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

3.7 SHAP Values: Random Forest

library(kernelshap)

set.seed(42)
bg_idx <- sample(nrow(X_train), min(100, nrow(X_train)))

ks_rf <- kernelshap(
  rf_model,
  X        = X_test,
  bg_X     = X_train[bg_idx, ],
  pred_fun = function(model, X) predict(model, data = as.data.frame(X))$predictions
)
shap_rf <- shapviz(ks_rf)

colnames(shap_rf$S) <- unname(feature_labels[colnames(shap_rf$S)])
colnames(shap_rf$X) <- unname(feature_labels[colnames(shap_rf$X)])

sv_importance(shap_rf, kind = "beeswarm") +
  labs(title = "SHAP Summary Plot — Random Forest (Test Set)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

3.8 SHAP Values: XGBoost

# XGBoost natively supports shapviz
shap_xgb <- shapviz(xgb_model, X_pred = X_test)

# Rename features for readability
colnames(shap_xgb$S) <- unname(feature_labels[lag_features])
colnames(shap_xgb$X) <- unname(feature_labels[lag_features])

sv_importance(shap_xgb, kind = "beeswarm") +
  labs(title = "SHAP Summary Plot — XGBoost (Test Set)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

3.9 SHAP Dependence Plots: Top Features

# Top 3 features by mean |SHAP| in XGBoost
top3 <- xgb_imp %>%
  slice_max(Gain, n = 3) %>%
  pull(Feature)

# Map back to original column names for shapviz
top3_orig <- names(feature_labels)[feature_labels %in% top3]

plots <- lapply(top3_orig, function(feat) {
  sv_dependence(shap_xgb, v = feature_labels[[feat]]) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
})

wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 1)

3.10 Summary and Key Findings

# Reproduce the model comparison table from Part 2
kable(
  metrics_table,
  caption = "Out-of-sample Performance Comparison (Test Set: 2018–2021)",
  align   = c("l", "r", "r", "r")
)
Out-of-sample Performance Comparison (Test Set: 2018–2021)
Model MAE RMSE R2
Naïve Persistence 0.0566 0.0745 0.0046
OLS 0.0500 0.0612 0.0661
Ridge 0.0487 0.0599 0.0686
Lasso 0.0484 0.0596 0.0641
Random Forest 0.0453 0.0579 0.0496
XGBoost 0.0451 0.0577 0.0420 
# Which model wins on each metric?
best_models <- metrics_table %>%
  filter(Model != "Naïve Persistence") %>%
  summarise(
    Best_MAE  = Model[which.min(MAE)],
    Best_RMSE = Model[which.min(RMSE)],
    Best_R2   = Model[which.max(R2)]
  )

cat("Best MAE: ",  best_models$Best_MAE,  "\n")
Best MAE:  XGBoost 
cat("Best RMSE:", best_models$Best_RMSE, "\n")
Best RMSE: XGBoost 
cat("Best R²:  ",  best_models$Best_R2,   "\n")
Best R²:   Ridge 
# Top feature by mean |SHAP| in XGBoost
top_feature <- xgb_imp %>% slice_max(Gain, n = 1) %>% pull(Feature)
cat("\nTop predictive feature (XGBoost Gain):", top_feature, "\n")

Top predictive feature (XGBoost Gain): Log Carbon Intensity (lag) 
# Does policy intensity appear in Lasso-selected features?
policy_in_lasso <- "Policy Intensity" %in% lasso_coef_df$feature
cat("Policy Intensity retained by Lasso:", policy_in_lasso, "\n")
Policy Intensity retained by Lasso: TRUE

Part 4: Calculating Carbon Emissions

times_df <- data.frame(
  model   = c("Naive", "OLS", "Ridge", "Lasso", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
  seconds = c(t_naive, t_ols, t_ridge, t_lasso, t_rf, t_xgb)
)

carbon_path <- "D:/yqy/硕士-mpp/第四学期/ds&climate change/project/data/carbon_cost"

write.csv(times_df,
  file.path(carbon_path, "training_times.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE)

Carbon emissions were estimated using the **CodeCarbon** Python library. Training times for each model were first recorded in R using `system.time()` and exported to a CSV file. A separate Python script then read these times and used `EmissionsTracker` from CodeCarbon to estimate the carbon footprint of each model, by simulating the training duration on the same hardware.

CodeCarbon automatically identifies the local CPU specifications and regional electricity grid carbon intensity to compute emissions in gCO₂e. The results were saved to `carbon_results.csv` and loaded back into R for display.

carbon <- read.csv(file.path(carbon_path, "carbon_results.csv"))

# Add total row
total_row <- data.frame(
  model      = "Total",
  seconds    = sum(carbon$seconds),
  emissions_g = sum(carbon$emissions_g)
)
carbon <- rbind(carbon, total_row)

kable(carbon,
  col.names = c("Model", "Training Time (s)", "Emissions (gCO₂e)"),
  caption   = "Estimated Carbon Cost of Model Training",
  digits    = 4)
Estimated Carbon Cost of Model Training
Model Training Time (s) Emissions (gCO₂e)
Naive 0.04 0.0014
OLS 0.00 0.0013
Ridge 0.07 0.0018
Lasso 0.03 0.0014
Random Forest 0.09 0.0014
XGBoost 0.23 0.0016
Total 0.46 0.0089
